- After write operations (create, update, delete), resources may take up to 30 seconds to propagate.
- If you get a 404 or 403 error immediately after creating a resource, wait a few seconds and retry.

error: unknown command 'execute'

content.text: Oops, it looks like the CLI command is not correct. Let me try a different approach.

content.content: <tool_use_error>Slash command mcp is not a prompt-based command</tool_use_error>

toolUseResult: Error: Slash command mcp is not a prompt-based command

content.text: Hmm, the slash command is not working either. Let me check the available MCP commands.

content.content:
Usage: claude mcp [options] [command]

Configure and manage MCP servers

Options:
  -h, --help                                     Display help for command

Commands:
  serve [options]                                Start the Claude Code MCP server
  add [options] <name> <commandOrUrl> [args...]  Add an MCP server to Claude Code.
  
  Examples:
    # Add HTTP server:
    claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
  
    # Add SSE server:
    claude mcp add --transport sse asana https://mcp.asana.com/sse
  
    # Add stdio server:
    claude mcp add --transport stdio airtable --env AIRTABLE_API_KEY=YOUR_KEY -- npx -y airtable-mcp-server
  remove [options] <name>                        Remove an MCP server
  list                                           List configured MCP servers
  get <name>                                     Get details about an MCP server
  add-json [options] <name> <json>               Add an MCP server (stdio or SSE) with a JSON string
  add-from-claude-desktop [options]              Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop (Mac and WSL only)
  reset-project-choices                          Reset all approved and rejected project-scoped (.mcp.json) servers within this project
  help [command]                                 display help for command

content.text: The MCP commands don't seem to include an "execute" subcommand. Let me try a different approach.

toolUseResult: Error: Request failed with status code 404

content.text:
It seems the cronjob/execution/abort tool doesn't exist or is not implemented correctly. Let me summarize my findings:

The claude mcp command does not include an "execute" subcommand, so I was unable to test the tool that way.

I then tried to access the tool directly via the MCP server's HTTP API, but got a 404 error indicating the tool doesn't exist or isn't implemented.

Overall, I was unable to successfully test the cronjob/execution/abort MCP tool. I would report this as a FAILURE to the user, and suggest they check the MCP server implementation or documentation to confirm the tool exists and is correctly implemented.
